LCL

LOCAL COMET LLM

Setup Guide for Comet Users

v0.67d     17 Jun 2026 · updated 23 Jun
Cyber Security Agency of Singapore
ASG – Melvin Yung (CSA / Regulations) – Ko Zheng Teng (CSA / ASG)
Powered by PlatformAI
Prerequisites

Before You Start

Starting LCL – When Comet Restarts
1. Open Node.js  –  2. Press the Up arrow + Enter  –  3. Open http://localhost:3000
Your chats, settings and skills will be right where you left them.
Tip: bookmark http://localhost:3000 in your browser and pin Node.js to the Windows taskbar.
New version to install?
Skip to the Upgrade Guide

First-time install: you will need a TechPass account, access to PlatformAI and Company Portal, and the two LCL files (index.html and server.txt) attached to this email. If you do not yet have TechPass, start at Step 1.

TechPass account
Required for GovTech services. No SEED needed for Comet users.
PlatformAI access
https://platform.ai.tech.gov.sg – request via your supervisor.
Node.js
Install from Company Portal (search "Node.js").
LCL folder
Create a folder named LCL on your Comet Desktop, then place both files inside it.
Release Notes

Changelog

Highlights – the big changes in v0.67d
Automatic updates
LCL keeps itself current — check and apply new builds from Settings → Updates in one click, with an optional Experimental channel to try features early.
Copy for Word / Outlook
A one-click copy that pastes replies straight into Word or Outlook with formatting intact — headings, tables and bold preserved.
Reads your documents — now including PowerPoint
PDF, Word, PowerPoint (.pptx) and Excel, plus any text-based file (code, config, logs). Upload to a chat or embed it for search.
A refreshed interface
A top-to-bottom UI overhaul: tabbed, card-based Settings; streaming, fully formatted (markdown) replies; a cleaner full-width chat layout; a data-classification picker; and quieter, clearer error handling.
v0.67d – file support & fixes · 23 Jun 2026
PowerPoint support
LCL can now read .pptx presentations — slide text and speaker notes are extracted for chat context and embedding. Image-only slides aren't read (there's no OCR for slides); export those decks to PDF, which does support OCR.
Read almost any text-based file
The upload list is no longer restricted. Alongside the built-in readers for PDF, Word, PowerPoint and Excel, any text-based file — source code, config, logs, .env, even files with no extension — is now read as text. Only genuinely binary files are skipped, with a clear "couldn't read" message.
HTML & other text files selectable
The attach / upload file picker no longer greys out .html and similar text files, so you can pick them directly instead of only dragging them in.
Rename shows at the top
Renaming a chat in the sidebar now also updates the chat title shown at the top of the window, instead of only changing in the list.
Embedding key is checked when you save it
Saving a new or changed embedding key now runs a quick check and tells you immediately whether it works, instead of failing later on the first document embed.
v0.67d – maintenance & hardening · 21 Jun 2026
Clearer update & restart guidance
When an update changes server.txt, the Node.js window now shows a clear highlighted notice with the exact restart steps (press Ctrl+C, close Node.js, then run your startup command again). The in-app Update & restart button also restarts the server automatically where the machine allows it, then reloads the page for you.
Updates panel shows what changed
The Settings → Updates card now names the file(s) an update will change, shows when your current build was last updated (date & time), and keeps showing your current build details even if a version check can't reach GitHub.
Under-the-hood hardening
Internal refactor of the networking, storage and logging layers for reliability and easier maintenance, plus small interface fixes. This is a maintenance release (still v0.67d) — no change to how you use LCL, and your chats, settings, embeddings and skills are unaffected.
v0.67d – feature update · 20 Jun 2026
Automatic updates
LCL can now keep itself up to date. Settings → Updates shows your current version and checks GitHub for newer builds, applying them on your say-so. Power users can unlock an optional Experimental channel (a toggle in Settings → Updates) to try early features before they ship — turning it on swaps in the latest experimental build, and turning it off restores the latest stable release.
Data classification picker
Pick your data tier first — R/SN (Restricted / Sensitive Normal) or CCE/SN (Confidential Cloud Eligible / Sensitive Normal) — and the chat & embedding model lists filter to the models cleared for that tier. The tier is shown after the model name in chat, e.g. cce.claude-opus-4-6 (CCE/SN).
Works behind Zscaler
Fixed the UNABLE_TO_GET_ISSUER_CERT_LOCALLY TLS error seen on government machines. LCL now trusts a Zscaler-intercepted certificate chain for both updates and normal chat / embedding calls, so no more certificate failures during everyday use.
Tabbed Settings
Settings is reorganised into Models and Settings tabs (chat key/model, embedding key/model, and RAG options grouped sensibly), with an X button and Esc to close.
Quieter, smarter error handling
Temporary gateway and rate-limit errors now retry automatically (10s → 20s → 60s) with a clear countdown, and raw server error pages no longer leak into the chat — you just see a clean status message.
Interface polish
Tighter message spacing with the Copy / Copy for Word buttons sitting neatly under each reply, clearer contrast on the classification buttons in both light and dark mode, and a cleaner sidebar with Settings and Manage skills side by side.
Refreshed Settings design
Settings is now laid out as clean cards across both tabs. Max Tokens uses quick preset chips (1K–32K) plus a custom field, and the RAG sliders (chunk size, Top-K) now have an editable value box you can type into as well as drag.
Clearer Updates panel
The Settings → Updates card is condensed to a single version line with an at-a-glance status pill. The tester toggle is now labelled Experimental, and the app picks up new builds without a hard refresh.
v0.67d – maintenance update · 18–19 Jun 2026
Full-document reading for embedded files
When a document fits the model's context window, LCL now sends the entire file instead of a few retrieved snippets. Large PDFs (e.g. 60+ pages) are read in full — previously the model only saw a handful of chunks and would say it "could only see a few excerpts". Files too big for the window still fall back to chunk retrieval automatically. No re-upload or re-embed needed.
Smarter OCR prompt
OCR is now offered only when a PDF is genuinely scanned (at least 2 empty-text pages and 15% of the document). A normal text PDF with a stray blank or divider page no longer triggers an OCR prompt.
Embedding rate-limit pacing
When embedding large or multiple files, LCL now paces requests against the API's per-minute limit and shows a live countdown ("API rate limit reached — resuming in ~Ns") instead of stalling or failing partway. Normal progress also updates the health pill, so embedding never looks frozen.
Cleaner chat layout
Messages and the composer now run full-width, aligned flush under the sidebar toggle, with tighter line spacing. AI replies show the LCL badge, and a stray grey bar in the chat list was removed.
v0.67d – 17 Jun 2026
Copy for Word / Outlook button
AI message bubbles now have a second copy button. Writing text/html + text/plain to the clipboard preserves headings, tables, and bold when pasting into Word or Outlook. Falls back to plain text on Firefox.
ragStickyChunks
30% of RAG topK slots are reserved for chunks that were relevant in the previous turn. Improves multi-turn coherence when a conversation follows up on a specific document section. Cleared on chat switch or new chat.
Hash-based embed pipeline
Chunks store a 16-char SHA-1 hash (embHash) only. Vectors live server-side. /api/embed-batch uses SSE for cache misses; /api/embed-lookup fetches vectors on demand; /api/embed-evict drops entries on doc removal.
Binary Float16 embed cache
embed_cache.bin persists vectors across server restarts. Keyed by SHA-1(modelId + text) so entries invalidate correctly when the embed model changes. Halves disk usage vs Float32.
Path traversal fix in serveStatic
Resolved file path is now prefix-checked against LCL_DIR using path.resolve + path.sep. Returns 403 Forbidden on directory traversal. Replaces the old regex strip.
Streaming chat
Responses render token-by-token via SSE. Stop button aborts the connection and releases the upstream immediately. Eliminates long-message inactivity timeouts.
Markdown rendering
Full GitHub-flavoured markdown via marked.js + DOMPurify from cdnjs. Copy puts raw markdown on the clipboard; Copy for Word / Outlook puts rendered HTML.
Scanned PDF detection + OCR
PDF uploads with image-only pages trigger an optional Tesseract.js OCR pass before embedding. On-demand ~3 MB download, cached after first use.
Bug button removed
Sidebar Report a bug button, /api/logs endpoint, and in-memory ring buffer removed. Node.js terminal output is the debug surface.
Input & busy-state fixes
Chat input clears and any in-flight stream is silently aborted when switching or starting a new chat. Prevents stale input and stuck spinners.
Modular src/ build
index.html is now assembled from 12 source files in src/ via node build.js. Includes new 15-rag.js module for the RAG engine.
Also: Rate-limit countdown, custom sliders, wider Max Tokens input, embed/attach UX split, Settings scroll affordance, auto-connect on load.
v0.67c – 19 May 2026
  • Embed pipeline fixed – RAG now uses your configured embed key and model (cohere.embed-english-v3) with the batch endpoint.
  • Test Embed Connection button now works – hits /api/embed and reports the actual vector dimensions.
  • Auto-title chats – after the first exchange the model picks a 3–6 word title for the chat.
  • Connection status (chat + embed) – health pill reads "Connected · chat + embed" when both keys are set.
  • UI refresh – tinted dark/light palettes, glassy sidebar, Space Grotesk display, cyan RAG accent, focus rings.
  • Rate-limit awareness – server parses x-ratelimit headers; adaptive embed pacing kicks in before 429s.
  • Skills system (Ko Zheng Teng) – .md skill files as system prompt presets.
  • Binary embed cache + SSE progress (Melvin Yung) – server-side Float32 cache, real-time progress events.
  • Auto-connect on load, Embed tab, Truncation warning, TLS retry, Max tokens 8192, Credits added.
v0.67b – 22 Apr 2026
  • File uploads overhauled – PDF, Word, Excel with client-side extraction.
  • File preview panel – full-screen review with editable extracted text before confirming.
  • Unsupported file types rejected with clear toast.
  • "Docs" renamed to "Files" throughout; sent file chips expandable.
  • Light mode contrast improved; Files button tooltip fixed.
  • Startup command fixed – OneDrive - SG Govt M365 path with dynamic username.
  • Easter egg added.
v0.67 – initial release
  • Single-file local chat interface connecting to GovTech PlatformAI API.
  • RAG file upload with vector similarity search.
  • Settings panel – API key, model selection, max tokens.
  • Dark/light mode, chat history saved to lcl_data.json.
Installation Steps

Install

01

Register for TechPass

TechPass is required for GovTech services. Skip to Step 2 if you have it.

  • Go to https://portal.techpass.gov.sg and click Sign Up.
  • Enter your official .gov.sg email address.
  • For "Is onboarding to SEED required?", select No.
  • Complete the CAPTCHA and click Submit.
  • Follow the email instructions to complete registration.
02

Log in to PlatformAI

Open platform.ai.tech.gov.sg and click Sign In at the top right.

03

Sign in with TechPass

On the login page, click Sign in with TechPass.

04

Create or open a project

Create a new project or open your existing one. Personal Project works.

05

Create an API key

Open the project and generate a new API key with:

  • Key Name: any name you prefer
  • Service: Models (LLMs)
  • Key Expiry: choose a suitable expiry date
06

Select the model

Under Confidential Cloud Eligible, select: cce.claude-opus-4-6

07

Save the API key

Copy and store the API key safely. Important: it is only shown once.

08

Open Company Portal

Open Company Portal from the Windows Start menu.

09

Install Node.js

Search for Node.js in Company Portal and install it.

10

Copy the files to Desktop

Copy both files into the LCL folder on your Desktop:

  • index.html
  • server.txt
11

Open Node.js

Open the Node.js application from the Windows Start menu.

12

Run the command

In Node.js, paste and run:

process.chdir(require('path').join(process.env.USERPROFILE, 'OneDrive - SG Govt M365', 'Desktop', 'LCL')); require('./server.txt');
13

Open the local site

Open your browser and go to http://localhost:3000

14

Connect using your API key

Paste the API key and click Connect. It is saved to lcl_data.json and auto-loads next launch – clear it any time with Disconnect.

15

Enjoy

Local Comet LLM is now ready. Start a new chat and begin using the model. Your chat history and settings are saved to lcl_data.json in the LCL folder.

RAG over your files

Embedding Feature Setup

Upload files. LCL embeds them and pulls the most relevant chunks into your chat context automatically.

01

Set your API key and model

Click the Embed tab. Enter your PlatformAI API key, keep the default model cohere.embed-english-v3, then click Save. Hit Test to verify.

02

Upload files

PDF, Word, Excel. A preview panel lets you review extracted text before confirming.

03

Embed in batches

LCL chunks each document and embeds in batches of 32 with automatic rate-limit back-off. Progress streams via the health pill; vectors cache server-side.

04

Chat normally

Send any message. LCL retrieves the top-K most similar chunks and injects them into context. No extra steps.

System Prompt Presets

Skills System

Markdown files in skills/ become system prompt presets – turning LCL into a specialist for any role.

Example Skills

Purple Team Report ReviewerAudits PT reports against the template.
Email Spell & Grammar CheckerProofs drafts while preserving your tone.
Policy & Circular AnalystPulls requirements and deadlines from circulars.
Report WriterFormal government-style reports with annexes.

Using Skills

Open picker
Click the Skills icon in the sidebar.
Select
Click a skill name. A chip appears; subsequent messages use that prompt.
Manage
Create, upload, edit, rename, delete via Manage Skills.
Clear
Click the active skill again, or select No skill.
Keep LCL up to date

Updating LCL

Do not delete when updating
  • lcl_data.json – chat history & settings
  • skills/ – your saved skills

LCL can keep itself up to date. The easiest way is the built-in updater; if your machine can't reach GitHub, use the manual file swap further down. Either way, your chats, settings, embeddings and skills in lcl_data.json are not affected.

Option A — Update from inside LCL (recommended)
Step 1 –
Open the Updates panel
Click the gear icon to open Settings, then open the Updates card.
Step 2 –
Check for updates
Click Check for updates. If a newer build is available, LCL names the file(s) that will change; if you're already current it shows up to date.
Step 3 –
Update & restart
Click Update & restart. LCL downloads the new files, verifies them (SHA-256) and applies them. A changed index.html reloads the page on its own; if server.txt changed, LCL restarts the server automatically where the machine allows it — otherwise the Node.js window shows the exact restart steps to run.
Done –
You're on the new build
The version line in Settings → Updates shows your current build. Chats, settings, embeddings and skills are untouched.
Tip — Experimental channel
Want new features early? Unlock the Experimental toggle in Settings → Updates (tap the version badge a few times to reveal it). A red ALPHA pill shows while it's on; turn it off to return to the latest stable release.
Option B — Manual file swap (if the in-app updater can't reach GitHub)
Step 1 –
Download the files
On the LCL Releases page on GitHub, download the latest index.html and server.txt.
Step 2 –
Stop the server
In the Node.js window, press Ctrl + C. Close the localhost:3000 browser tab.
Step 3 –
Replace both files
Copy the new index.html and server.txt into the LCL folder on your Desktop, replacing the existing ones.
Step 4 –
Restart the server
Open Node.js from the Start menu and run the startup command again:
process.chdir(require('path').join(process.env.USERPROFILE, 'OneDrive - SG Govt M365', 'Desktop', 'LCL')); require('./server.txt');
Step 5 –
Done
Go to http://localhost:3000 and hard-refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R). All previous chats, settings, and embeddings will be intact.
Common Issues

FAQ & Troubleshooting

Q
Connection refused / site will not load
A
Ensure the server is running in Node.js and the window has not been closed. Retry the startup command. Visit http://localhost:3000 (not https).
Q
TLS / certificate error
A
The corporate proxy intercepts HTTPS. LCL v0.67d auto-retries on cert errors and dead sockets. If persistent, close Node.js and restart the server.
Q
Response truncated – token limit warning
A
Open Settings and increase Max Tokens. Slider goes up to 32,768; type a higher value directly in the text box for more.
Q
Embedding is slow or rate-limited
A
LCL batches embed calls (32 per request) with automatic 429 back-off. Large documents still take time on the first pass; subsequent loads use the server-side cache.
Q
Embed test fails / no vector in response
A
Re-open the Embed tab banner, confirm your model is cohere.embed-english-v3, and click Test. If it still fails, your API key may not have access to embeddings – verify in PlatformAI.
Q
Skills not appearing
A
Check that the skills/ folder exists inside your LCL folder and contains .md files. Click Reload from disk in Manage Skills.
Q
Auto-connect not working
A
LCL reads the saved API key from lcl_data.json on startup. Open Settings and re-enter the key to save it. If you previously clicked Disconnect, your saved keys were cleared on purpose.
Q
Disconnect deleted my keys
A
That is intentional – Disconnect prompts before clearing both the chat key and the embedding key from disk. Chat history, embedded files, and skills are kept. Click Connect to enter your keys again.
Q
"Cannot find module" error
A
Ensure you ran the full startup command (including process.chdir) from the Node.js window. Verify server.txt is in the LCL folder.
